name: Auto Memory Index - JASON-OS
purpose: Master index of all user-home memories for this project, organized by section (User, Feedback, Project, Reference) with one-line summaries and links to individual files.
notes: The index itself has no frontmatter. The canonical version exists but likely diverges (user-home index references ~60 files vs canonical's ~12). Contains references to files not yet seen: user_creation_mindset, project_sync_mechanism_principles, t3_convergence_loops, feedback_statusline_rebuild_safety, and many others not in canonical.

name: Acknowledgment actions require explicit approval
purpose: Prohibits Claude from acknowledging warnings, clearing state, or modifying ack files without explicit user approval — even when approval seems likely.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Never acknowledge warnings, clear state, or modify ack files without explicit user approval — even when user seems likely to approve

notes: Not in canonical. Closely related to feedback_interactive_gates but specifically about ack/warning state files. Distinct enough to merit its own entry. High-value behavioral guardrail.

name: Agent config file revert hazard
purpose: Warns that spawned agents reading shared config files (e.g., .claude/settings.json) at spawn time may silently overwrite changes made by the main session earlier.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Spawned agents can silently revert unrelated changes in shared config files like .claude/settings.json — always verify after agent edits

notes: Not in canonical. High-value platform-behavior finding. Portable — applies anywhere Claude agents edit shared config files. Specific to .claude/settings.json but principle is general.

name: Agent hot-reload doesn't exist
purpose: Documents that Claude Code loads .claude/agents/ only at session start — agents added mid-session are invisible and require a session restart.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Claude Code loads .claude/agents/ at session start — new agents added mid-session are invisible until restart

notes: Not in canonical. Discovered 2026-04-15 during JASON-OS bootstrap. Platform-level behavior fact — highly portable, not project-specific. Has originSessionId field (richer frontmatter than most).

name: Agent output files always empty on Windows
purpose: Documents Windows-specific bug where background Agent output_file is always 0 bytes without Developer Mode (symlink restriction); prescribes using task-notification result field instead.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_name: Agent output files always empty on Windows
existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Background Agent output_file is always 0 bytes on Windows without Developer Mode — use task-notification result, never silently fail

notes: Not in canonical. Machine-scoped: Windows-only bug (issue #39791). Home locale has Developer Mode; work locale cannot. The mitigation pattern (check task-notification result) is portable but the root cause is Windows-specific. Should tag as machine-scoped but keep the mitigation pattern portable.

name: Agent stalling on large input
purpose: Documents that synthesis agents reading 16+ large files silently stall due to context exhaustion; prescribes capping input at 10-12 files per agent and using multiple narrower-scoped agents.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Agents reading 16+ findings files silently stall (context exhaustion, no error). Fix — split into narrower-scoped agents.

notes: Not in canonical. Platform-level behavior — applies to any deep-research or synthesis agent workflow. Highly portable.

name: feedback_agent_teams_learnings
purpose: Operational learnings from first-hand agent team usage: Explore is read-only, idle notification flood, token cost 3-7x solo, optimal task sizing, subagent vs team decision rule, one team per session limit.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Agent teams operational learnings — Explore read-only, idle floods, token cost, team sizing, subagent rules

notes: HAS canonical counterpart. System-reminder notes memory is 2 days old. Frontmatter name uses filename slug (not cleaned display name) — minor quality issue. Status field present ('active').

name: feedback_code_review_patterns
purpose: Code review and PR workflow learnings: never reject as pre-existing, detailed PR descriptions, create PR after session close-out, commit all review rounds before pushing.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Code review and PR workflow learnings — rejection policy, description quality, bot sequencing

notes: HAS canonical counterpart. System-reminder notes memory is 2 days old. Frontmatter name is filename slug. Status field present ('active'). Note overlap with feedback_no_preexisting_rejection (canonical) — code_review_patterns is broader.

name: Commit hook state files with other commits
purpose: Reminds Claude to include hook state files (.claude/state/, override-log.jsonl, AI_REVIEW_LEARNINGS_LOG.md) in commits because they accumulate silently and get forgotten.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_name: Commit hook state files with other commits
existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Hook state files (.claude/state/, .claude/override-log.jsonl, docs/AI_REVIEW_LEARNINGS_LOG.md) accumulate unstaged changes and get forgotten — include them in any commit

notes: Not in canonical. References JASON-OS-specific file paths (.claude/state/hook-warnings-log.jsonl etc.) making it project-scoped. The principle (don't let state files pile up) is portable but the specific file list is not. Sanitize-then-portable for SoNash adaptation.

name: feedback_convergence_loops_mandatory
purpose: Mandates that every significant pass of work use internal convergence loops (min 2 iterations); documents cascade failure from skipping loops in prior sessions.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Every pass of significant work must loop internally until converged. Shortcuts cascade.

notes: HAS canonical counterpart. System-reminder notes memory is 2 days old. Frontmatter name is filename slug. Status field present ('active').

name: feedback_deep_plan_hook_discovery_process
purpose: Specifies a 5-layer parallel discovery process for deep-plans involving hooks, scripts, pipelines, or infrastructure: subsystem explorers, warning infra, skip reasons, ground-truth verifier, execution verifier.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: 5-layer multi-agent discovery for hook/infrastructure deep-plans — subsystem explorers, warning infra, skip reasons, ground-truth, execution verification

notes: Not in canonical. Methodology memory — entirely portable. No project-specific references. Status field present ('active').

name: Deep-plan Q&A format for ad-hoc clarifying questions
purpose: Requires Claude to use the deep-plan skill's Q&A format (batched, recommended defaults, phase markers) for ANY non-trivial clarifying questions — not just formal /deep-plan invocations.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_name: Deep-plan Q&A format for ad-hoc clarifying questions
existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: When Claude needs to ask clarifying questions for a non-trivial fix, use the deep-plan skill's Q&A format (batches, recommended defaults, category sub-themes) even when not formally invoking /deep-plan.

notes: Not in canonical. Largest of the 21 files (2556 bytes). Has originSessionId field. References .claude/skills/deep-plan/SKILL.md — portable if skill exists in target. Contains cross-references to other memory files. High-value behavioral rule.

name: Deep-plan Phase 0 research check is MANDATORY
purpose: Mandates that /deep-plan Phase 0 Step 3 always include prior research lookup and offer to run /deep-research first — corrected 3 times in one session.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_name: Deep-plan Phase 0 research check is MANDATORY
existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Never skip the /deep-research offer in deep-plan Phase 0 Step 3 — corrected 3 times in one session

notes: Not in canonical. References .research/ path convention — portable. Skill-methodology memory. Very clear 4-step prescription. High-value guardrail.

name: Deep-research formula insufficient for large repos
purpose: Documents that the D+3+floor(D/5) agent allocation formula is a floor, not a ceiling, and must be supplemented with scope assessment and presentation of both formula and recommended counts to user.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_name: Deep-research formula insufficient for large repos
existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Agent allocation formula D+3+floor(D/5) produces too few agents for large codebase analysis tasks requiring contextual understanding

notes: Not in canonical. References two specific sessions (#235 and #244) with concrete numbers. Highly portable methodology rule. Mentions gap-fill, verification, and challenge agents as separate layers beyond formula.

name: Deep-research Phases 3-5 are MANDATORY
purpose: Prohibits skipping deep-research Phases 3-5 (CL verification, cross-model Gemini checks, self-audit, presentation with routing menu) — documents session where skipping cost all 179 claims' trustworthiness.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Never skip CL verification, cross-model checks, self-audit, or presentation in deep-research. Corrected Session #235 — entire research scrapped.

notes: Not in canonical. References Gemini CLI cross-model verification — external tool dependency but portable concept. Mentions 179 claims scrapped in Session #235 as concrete consequence. High-stakes guardrail.

name: Don't over-surface side tracks
purpose: Instructs Claude to answer 'anything else?' queries with only genuine blockers — not enumerating all deferred items, filed follow-ups, or informational warnings that have no immediate action.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: When user asks 'anything else?' or similar, answer tight — don't enumerate every deferred item, filed follow-up, and informational warning

notes: Not in canonical. Contains a direct user quote ('JESUS i just want to get back to the synthesis plan. PLEASE') providing emotional context for the rule. Pure behavioral rule — fully portable.

name: feedback_execution_failure_recovery
purpose: Failure recovery discipline: stop and diagnose root cause before retrying, verify diagnosis before acting, avoid --no-verify and destructive shortcuts, ask user when blocked.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Failure recovery discipline — stop-diagnose-confirm before retrying, no destructive shortcuts

notes: HAS canonical counterpart. System-reminder notes memory is 2 days old. Frontmatter name is filename slug. Status field present ('active'). Smallest of the files with canonical counterpart (698 bytes).

name: Explain before decide (layman's terms, Q&A format)
purpose: Requires Claude to explain technical terms and options in plain language before asking user to make decisions — user is a no-code orchestrator who needs context before deciding.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_name: Explain before decide (layman's terms, Q&A format)
existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: User is a non-coder orchestrator. Never present a technical decision without plain-language explanation of each option and its tradeoffs. Use deep-plan Q&A format.

notes: Not in canonical. Has originSessionId field. User-scoped because it describes a specific user's expertise profile. Cross-references 3 other memories. Contains explicit 'What this is NOT' section — well-structured. The principle is universal but the trigger condition (no-code orchestrator) is user-specific.

name: Grep lacks intelligence — use structured data where possible
purpose: Documents three anti-patterns for grep: don't use for analysis, replace grep pipelines with git pathspec or JSON+jq, don't use for verification (prescribes end-to-end testing instead).

existing_metadata.frontmatter_name: Grep lacks intelligence — use structured data where possible
existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Grep is dumb string matching. For analysis use Read; for machine parsing replace grep pipelines with JSON+jq

notes: Not in canonical. Canonical has feedback_verify_not_grep.md which likely overlaps but covers different angle (this covers 3 distinct anti-patterns; canonical probably focuses on verification). Has originSessionId. References CC pre-push gate as repeated verification failure pattern.

name: Wait for user at interactive gates
purpose: Three-part rule: choice prompts are hard stops (wait for response), never act on a question you just asked, never interpret ambiguous input as bulk acceptance.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_name: Wait for user at interactive gates
existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: When presenting interactive prompts (Skip/Analyze/Select), MUST stop and wait for user response before continuing

notes: Not in canonical. Same originSessionId as feedback_grep_vs_understanding — from same session. Overlaps with feedback_ack_requires_approval (rule 2 is identical) and feedback_never_bulk_accept (rule 3 is identical). This file is a superset combining three related rules. Could be split in canonical or consolidated here.

name: Never bulk-accept discovery answers
purpose: Prohibits Claude from interpreting a single ambiguous response as acceptance of an entire batch of discovery questions — each question needs its own explicit answer.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Never interpret ambiguous input as bulk acceptance of multiple questions. Each question needs an explicit answer.

notes: Not in canonical. Content substantially overlaps with feedback_interactive_gates.md rule 3. Both cover the same 'user said A and Claude bulk-accepted all 7 questions' incident. Redundancy risk — consolidation candidate during canonicalization.
